pattern: Rotate a list rightward by k by reducing k modulo the length and re-cutting the chain at the new boundary.
hints: Rotating by the length (or any multiple of it) returns the original list, so only k modulo the length actually matters.
First walk the list to learn its length, then connect the tail to the head to form a ring.
From the old tail, step forward length - (k mod length) nodes; that node becomes the new tail, and the node after it is the new head — break the ring there.

edge_inputs.description: k larger than length must be reduced modulo length
very large k near the cap

stress.description: long random list with a large random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, min(size, 500))
    arr = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 2000000000)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: k may far exceed length, exercising the modulo reduction
